zh.lifeSummary: 當George Tucker, of Throwleigh and Gravesend於1532年4月出生在英國肯特米爾頓時，他的父親William Tucker37歲，母親Isota Josea Ashe35歲。他於1563年與Maria Hunter在英國肯特結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和6個女兒的父母。他去世於1587年3月31日享年54歲，埋葬在英國肯特格拉福森德。

nameMeanings.name: Tucker,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southwestern): occupational name from Middle English tuker(e), toker(e) ‘tucker, fuller’, a derivative of tuken ‘to torment, beat’ (Old English tūcian), for someone who fulled and finished cloth. This name for the occupation was characteristic of the West Country. Compare Fuller and Walker and see also Tuckerman .
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Tuachair ‘descendant of Tuachar’, a personal name composed of the elements tuath ‘people’ + car ‘dear, beloved’.
Americanized form of Jewish Tocker or Toker (see Tokarz ).

variants.name: Tooker
meanings: English (Devon) and Irish: variant of   Tucker  .

variants.name: Tuckerman
meanings: English: from Middle English tuker(e), toker(e) ‘tucker, fuller’ (see Tucker 1) + -man, either for a tucker, or for his servant or assistant.

variants.name: Fuller
meanings: English: occupational name for a dresser of cloth, from Middle English fuller ‘fuller of cloth’ (partly from Old English fullere, partly from Old French fouleor, foleur, Latin fullo). Raw cloth had to be fulled, i.e. scoured, cleansed, and thickened by beating or trampling it in water, a process also known as walking or tucking, hence the surnames Walker and Tucker alongside Fuller. These three terms and surnames are characteristic of different parts of England. In general, in Middle English, Fuller is southern and eastern, while Walker belongs to the west and north and Tucker is southwestern. Compare Fullen .
English: variant of Fullard with loss of -d.
German (Füller): occupational name for a person whose work involved filling, such as a dauber, or a nickname for a gourmand or glutton. Compare Filler .

variants.name: Swinger
meanings: English: occupational name, perhaps for a fuller, who finished cloth by beating it and stretching it on tenters. Middle English swyngere is not independently recorded but it appears to be an agent derivative of Middle English swingen ‘to beat, strike, punish, afflict (something)’. Compare the synonymous Tucker , an agent derivative of Middle English tuken ‘to torment, beat’. The earliest bearers of the name Swinger were wealthy townsmen.
In some instances probably an Americanized form of German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Schwinger , or German Zwinger, a nickname from Middle High German zwinger ‘oppressor’.

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When George Tucker, of Throwleigh and Gravesend was born in April 1532, in Milton, Kent, England, his father, William Tucker, was 37 and his mother, Isota Josea Ashe, was 35. He married Maria Hunter in 1563, in Kent, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 6 daughters. He died on 31 March 1587, at the age of 54, and was buried in Gravesend, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Tucker, of Throwleigh and Gravesend est né en avril 1532, à Milton, Kent, Angleterre, son père, William Tucker, avait 37 ans et sa mère, Isota Josea Ashe, avait 35 ans. Il a épousé Maria Hunter en 1563, à Kent, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 6 filles. Il est décédé le 31 mars 1587, à l’âge de 54 ans, et a été enterré à Gravesend, Kent, Angleterre.

Er hat 1563 in Kent, England Maria Hunter geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 6 Töchter. Er starb am 31. März 1587 im Alter von 54 Jahren und wurde in Gravesend, Kent, England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: George Tucker, of Throwleigh and Gravesend(이)가 1532년 4월 영국 켄트 미턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Tucker(은)는 37세였고, 그의 어머니 Isota Josea Ashe(은)는 35세였다. 그는 1563년 영국 켄트에서 Maria Hunter(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 여섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1587년 3월 31일 54세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 그레이베센드에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Tucker, of Throwleigh and Gravesend родился апрель 1532 г. в местности милтон, кэнт, Англия, его отцу по имени William Tucker было 37 лет, а матери, которую звали Isota Josea Ashe, 35. Он женился на женщине по имени Maria Hunter в 1563 г. в местности кэнт, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 6 дочерей. Он умер 31 марта 1587 г. в возрасте 54 лет и был похоронен в местности гравесенд, кэнт, Англия.
